page: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb is a memory-optimized virtual machine within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The host allocates 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core on a shared CPU basis. The system features 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and there are no onboard GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieved a score of 3621.37, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also performed in the top tier, with gzip compression reaching 39.66 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.59 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is more constrained, likely due to the shared CPU allocation. The multi-threaded bzip3 decompression score of 35.07 GB/sec falls into the bottom 25%, and the stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 7237.76 is in the average tier. Memory bandwidth remains stable, with read-only large block transfers at 69.51 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 41.08 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation model offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive applications that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU execution. The high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM paired with 4 shared vCPUs makes this instance suitable for memory-cached databases, Redis deployments, and lightweight web serving. Redis SET operations reached an average tier performance of 8.06 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput was measured at 14.6 GB/sec. It is less suited for heavy multi-threaded processing or large-scale LLM inference, as demonstrated by a poor prompt processing speed of 15.98 tokens/sec on a 7B model.
description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb is a memory-optimized virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, it offers a high 8.0 GB per core memory density, paired with a minimal 1 GB network-attached local storage volume and no GPU. Single-core performance is strong, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks ranking in the top 10%. However, the shared CPU allocation results in average multi-core performance and a weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking. This resource profile provides a cost-effective solution for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is best suited for caching layers, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb is a memory-optimized server with 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-4c-32gb features 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, offering efficient single-core performance for memory-heavy applications.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high memory density with strong single-core performance for cost-effective caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB network-attached local storage with no GPU hardware
Best suited for Redis deployments, caching, and database operations
